title: Separating Hyperplane Theorem for Convex Sets
description: A visual explanation of how a hyperplane can separate two disjoint convex sets, including the key elements and their spatial relationships.
relationships: Sets A and B are disjoint convex sets
Hyperplane H separates the space into two half-spaces
Normal vector w is perpendicular to H and defines the separation direction
All points in A lie on one side of H (w^T x >= b)
All points in B lie on the other side of H (w^T x <= b)
